executiveSummary: Two confirmed injection vulnerabilities in a public Express API: untrusted req.query values reach a raw SQL query and a shell command across files, with no validation. Both are directly exploitable by any client.

remediations.fix: Use execFile with an argv array; never build a shell string from input.
Use a parameterized query (placeholders), never string concatenation.
Use execFile with an argv array; never build a shell string from input.

attackChains.title: Unauthenticated SQL injection via /user
attackChains.narrative: GET /user?id=... flows req.query.id across server.js into a string-concatenated SQL query in db.getUser.

rootCauses.cause: Untrusted request input concatenated into interpreters
rootCauses.note: Centralize input handling: parameterized queries + argv-array exec, plus an input-validation layer.
